title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE RURAL HEALTH UNIT IN THE CITY OF IRIGA, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR, INTO A FIVE-BED CAPACITY HEALTH INFIRMARY, TO BE KNOWN AS THE IRIGA HEALTH INFIRMARY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. NO. 15925 / 88 OG No. 22, 3271 (June 1, 1992)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7385, April 10, 1992 ]

AN ACT CONVERTING THE RURAL HEALTH UNIT IN THE CITY OF IRIGA, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR, INTO A FIVE-BED CAPACITY HEALTH INFIRMARY, TO BE KNOWN AS THE IRIGA HEALTH INFIRMARY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The rural health unit in the City of Iriga, Province of Camarines Sur, is hereby converted into a five-bed capacity health infirmary, to be known as the Iriga Health Infirmary.

SEC. 2. The amount necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act shall be included in the General Appropriations Act of the year following its enactment into law and thereafter.

SEC. 3. The existing and available facilities, equipment, and hospital staff may be increased subject to the approval of the Department of Health, to meet required services.

SEC. 4. This Act shall take effect immediately upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) NEPTALI A. GONZALES

(Sgd.)

RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 2, 1991 and February 3, 1992, respectively.

(Sgd.)

ANACLETO D. BADOY, JR.

(Sgd.)

CAMILO L. SABIO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: April 10, 1992

(Sgd.)

CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
